cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A process of developing a program by repeatedly making small improvements and testing each change

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A process of developing a program by adding one feature at a time, testing each feature before moving on

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Working together with others to write or improve a program

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A development practice where two programmers share one workstation — one writes code while the other reviews each line

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A systematic examination of another person's code intended to find and fix mistakes or improve quality

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A system that records changes to files over time, allowing you to recall specific versions later

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: An error in a program caused by incorrect grammar or syntax rules of the programming language

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: An error that occurs while a program is running, causing it to crash or behave unexpectedly

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: An error where a program runs without crashing but produces incorrect results due to flawed reasoning

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: An error that occurs when a calculation produces a result too large to be stored in the allotted memory space

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: The process of finding and fixing errors (bugs) in a program

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: An innovation that uses a computing device or system to accomplish a goal — can be physical, nonphysical, or both

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Program purpose
cards.answer: Why a program exists — the problem it is intended to solve or the goal it is meant to achieve

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Program function
cards.answer: What a program actually does — its specific behaviors and actions when executed

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Prototype
cards.answer: A working model of a program that demonstrates the basic design and intended functionality before full development

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Specification
cards.answer: A detailed description of what a program is supposed to do — its inputs, outputs, and expected behavior

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Documentation
cards.answer: Written text that accompanies a program to explain its purpose, design, or how to use it

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A student's program crashes and displays an error message while it is running. What type of error is this?

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A student writes a program that compiles without problems, but displays the wrong total when adding numbers. What type of error is this?

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A student forgets a closing brace in their program and the program will not run at all. What type of error is this?

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A physical computing innovation that has a tangible form, such as a robot, smart device, or embedded system

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A computing innovation that exists only as software or a digital service, with no physical form

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: The part of a program that a user can see and interact with

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A single, complete instruction in a programming language

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A group of related code statements that work together to accomplish a task

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Event (in computing)
cards.answer: An action or occurrence that can be detected by a program, such as a button click or keypress, which triggers a response

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Information that a program takes in from the user or environment to process

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Information that a program sends to a user or environment as a result of its processing

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A developer builds a basic version of an app with only the login screen working, tests it, then adds the search feature, tests it, and so on one feature at a time. What development approach is this?

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A developer builds a full draft of an app, reviews user feedback, revises the design, and releases an improved version repeatedly. What development approach is this?

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: In computing, the way a program behaves in response to inputs — what the program does

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Acknowledgment (in collaborative development)
cards.answer: Credit given to those who contributed to or inspired a program, including sources, libraries, and collaborators used

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A set of instructions for completing a task that can be implemented as a program

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: A set of steps for developing a program that typically includes planning, designing, coding, testing, and refining

cards.subject: ap-csp
cards.prompt: Two benefits of collaboration during program development
cards.answer: Diverse perspectives and catching errors earlier
